mechanism_narrative: Steady growth in nonfarm payrolls signals expanding economic activity and rising corporate earnings, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Any 2-year rolling window where a 1% increase in PAYEMS is followed by negative average 20-day S&P 500 returns.

mechanism_narrative: A drop in the unemployment rate below 8% from elevated post-crisis levels confirms labor market healing, boosting consumer confidence and equity valuations over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: After UNRATE drops below 8%, the average 20-day S&P 500 return is not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Low federal funds rates combined with rising industrial production indicate accommodative monetary policy supporting real activity, jointly forecasting positive S&P 500 returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: The composite z-score of (INDPRO growth minus FEDFUNDS) has a 3-year rolling rank IC below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: In a near-zero interest rate environment, rising inflation signals potential monetary tightening, which depresses equity prices over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS < 0.25, a 1% rise in CPIAUCSL leads to positive average 20-day S&P 500 returns.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts reflect robust residential investment and consumer demand, leading to positive short-term S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: A 10% increase in HOUST is followed by negative average 5-day S&P 500 returns over any 3-year period.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in industrial production signals weakening manufacturing activity, which negatively impacts investor sentiment and forward S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: After an INDPRO decline, the 20-day S&P 500 return is positive in more than 55% of cases over a 5-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in real GDP combined with rising unemployment confirms a recessionary demand shock, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the following quarter.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter decline and UNRATE quarter-over-quarter increase
failure_condition: When GDPC1 declines and UNRATE rises in the same quarter, the average 60-day S&P 500 return is not negative or fails a one-sided t-test at 10% significance.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales exceeding $400 billion indicate strong consumer spending, which lifts corporate revenues and stock prices within the following week.
failure_condition: After RSAFS exceeds 400,000, the average 5-day S&P 500 return is not positive or fails a binomial test with p > 0.10.
